talk_metadata.talk_title: Plasma proteomic signature for persistent smoking effects and cancer risk among former smokers
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we further excluded the participants who had the cancer history or who developed the cancer during the follow-up time"
Speaker said: "adding our signature to the PLCO 2012 models significantly improved the predictive performance"

key_data_gaps: No external validation in non-UK Biobank cohorts beyond small Shanghai case‑control
No OS or cancer‑specific mortality data presented

crispro_opportunity.description: Proteomic signature could be combined with CrisPRO's computational risk modeling to improve lung cancer screening in long‑term quitters.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "adding our signature to the PLCO 2012 models significantly improved the predictive performance, especially among long‑term quitters"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate proteomic scores with genetic and clinical features to refine individual risk predictions.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: check ongoing lung cancer screening trials for biomarker integration
PubMed: validation of proteomic signatures in diverse cohorts

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health
talk_metadata.talk_title: Adult height and fatal prostate cancer risk in the Health Professionals Follow‑Up Study
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the association between height and fatal prostate cancer is mostly likely to be driven by the genetic components of height"
Speaker said: "the residual of height after accounting for the PRS showed essentially no association"

key_data_gaps: No functional validation of height‑associated pathways

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO could model how height‑related genetic risk interacts with androgen signaling pathways to predict aggressive prostate cancer.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted the androgen response pathway as up‑regulated in taller individuals' tumors.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate gene‑environment interactions linking polygenic height scores to pathway activity.
external_validation_needed: PubMed: studies linking height PRS to androgen signaling
ClinicalTrials.gov: prostate cancer trials stratified by genetic risk

unresolved_questions: How do specific height‑associated genetic variants mechanistically drive androgen pathway activation?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Copy number alteration subtypes in high‑grade serous ovarian carcinoma and racial survival disparities
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "cluster membership itself did not differ by race"
Speaker said: "within the gain dominant cluster, Black patients have higher hazard of death compared to white patients"

key_data_gaps: Copy number inferred from methylation arrays rather than SNP genotyping

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO could help design trials that stratify HSOC patients by CNA subtype and ancestry to avoid signal dilution.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted survival disparities concentrated within the gain‑dominant CNA cluster for Black women.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate trial outcomes under different stratification schemes using CNA and ancestry inputs.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: HSOC trials with CNA‑based eligibility
PubMed: validation of methylation‑derived CNA calls

unresolved_questions: What molecular mechanisms drive poorer survival of Black patients within the gain‑dominant CNA cluster?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Spatial context of T‑cell infiltration and exhaustion predicts ovarian cancer survival in Black women
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "higher cytotoxic T cells in the tumor are associated with better survival, whereas in the stroma they are associated with worse survival"
Speaker said: "tumor‑specific T‑cell exhaustion was associated with improved survival"

key_data_gaps: Lack of validation in non‑Black cohorts
No functional assays confirming exhaustion phenotype

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO could model how spatial immune metrics interact with checkpoint inhibitor response in ovarian cancer.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted that tumor‑specific exhaustion correlated with better survival.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate spatial immunophenotype data with drug response predictions.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: ovarian cancer immunotherapy trials with spatial profiling
PubMed: studies linking TIM‑3/PD‑1 expression to outcomes

unresolved_questions: Why does exhaustion correlate with better survival only in the tumor compartment?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Biologic processes enriched in primary breast tumors with disseminated tumor cells
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "DTC‑positive patients were more likely to have somatic mutations in Wnt/β‑catenin, PI3K/AKT/mTOR, and NRF2 pathways"
Speaker said: "APOBEC mutagenesis activity was nearly twofold higher in DTC‑positive patients"

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO could develop a composite risk model combining Wnt/PI3K mutations and APOBEC signature to predict DTC‑driven recurrence.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted distinct pathway alterations in DTC‑positive tumors.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate mutational signatures with transcriptomic data to generate recurrence risk scores.
external_validation_needed: ClinicalTrials.gov: trials collecting bone‑marrow DTC data
PubMed: validation of APOBEC signature as recurrence predictor

unresolved_questions: What determines whether a DTC‑positive patient will progress to overt metastasis?

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health
talk_metadata.talk_title: Germline genetic impact on colorectal cancer risk across birth cohorts
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the association between PRS and CRC is generally increasing in more recent birth cohorts"
Speaker said: "innate immune system was the top enriched pathway in gene set enrichment analysis"

key_data_gaps: No direct measurement of environmental exposures per birth cohort

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO can model how CRC PRS interacts with birth‑cohort specific environmental factors to refine risk prediction.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted stronger PRS‑CRC association in recent birth cohorts and unscreened individuals.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO integrates polygenic scores with temporal exposure data to simulate cohort‑specific risk.
external_validation_needed: PubMed: studies on environmental risk factors by birth cohort
ClinicalTrials.gov: CRC screening trials with genetic risk stratification

unresolved_questions: What specific environmental exposures drive the observed birth‑cohort effect?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Functional characterization of STARD13 as a BRCA2 modifier in breast cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "RS3742324 region exhibits allele‑specific enhancer activity"
Speaker said: "STARD13 knockdown leads to greater down‑regulation of LATAS2 in BRCA2 mutant cells"

key_data_gaps: No in‑vivo validation of STARD13 as a modifier

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO could prioritize functional non‑coding variants at the STARD13 locus for further validation, rescuing the AlphaGenome candidates.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted that AlphaGenome‑prioritized variants showed no enhancer activity in assays.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO uses deep learning to predict regulatory impact, guiding experimental focus.
external_validation_needed: PubMed: functional assays of non‑coding BRCA2 modifiers
ClinicalTrials.gov: trials of BRCA2‑targeted therapies incorporating modifier status

unresolved_questions: How does STARD13 modulation affect therapeutic response in BRCA2‑mutant breast cancer?
